reasoning: The Lean formalization carefully encodes all components of the game: (1) GameString n = Fin n → Fin 3 represents strings of n digits in {0,1,2}; (2) initialState is all zeros; (3) isValidMove requires exactly one differing position (via ∃!) where the digits differ by ±1 using val-based arithmetic, correctly forbidding 0↔2 jumps; (4) IsValidGamePlay uses List.Chain from initialState plus Nodup on the full trajectory (including initial state), matching "new string not appeared before"; (5) HasWinningStrategy is an inductive (least fixed point) definition that correctly captures "player-to-move can win" for finite games — vacuous when opponent has no reply (win), unprovable when the current player has no move (loss); (6) AliceHasWinningStrategy n = HasWinningStrategy n [] places Alice as first mover; (7) the hypothesis 1 ≤ n matches "For each n ≥ 1"; (8) the theorem asks putnam_2025_a3_solution n ↔ AliceHasWinningStrategy n, faithfully asking to characterize when Alice wins. All definitions align with standard game-theoretic meaning.
